title: Frequency
short: How many cycles happen per second, f = 1/T, measured in hertz (Hz).
full:
Frequency f counts how many complete cycles of a wave or oscillation occur each second, f = 1/T, where T is the period. One hertz is one cycle per second.

For light, frequency fixes the colour and, through E = h·f, the energy of each photon; for sound it fixes the pitch. Frequency does not change when a wave passes between media, even though its speed and wavelength do.

The SI unit is the hertz (Hz).
